articles.title: Fed's Williams: Trims his GDP forecast and boosts inflation view

articles.title: Eurozone: Higher inflation and ECB hikes support Euro – BNP Paribas
articles.expand: BNP Paribas economists project Eurozone Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth to slow from 1.5% in 2025 to 1.0% in 2026, with inflation rebounding to 3.0% in 2026 and 3.3% in 2027.

articles.title: A source tells CNN that Dubai expect US/Israeli strikes on Iran within the next 24 hours
articles.expand: It's all coming undone today. It looks like Iran sensed that something was coming and decided to strike first. The UAE's oil export facilities were its first target as it tries to maintain control of the Strait of Hormuz. There are also videos of a cargo ship on fire. The US attempted to break the blockade today but it looks like that triggered a r...

articles.title: Gold extends declines as escalating Middle East tensions lift the US Dollar.
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USDS) kicks off the week under pressure, hovering near one-month lows as hawkish interest rate expectations continue to weigh on the non-yielding metal amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East.

articles.title: GBP/USD recoils as Hormuz risks lift US Dollar and WTI
articles.expand: GBP/USD falls on Monday as tensions in the Middle East remain high, as Iran threatens the US Navy not to enter the Strait of Hormuz, amid growing speculation that Tehran launched missiles at a US warship. At the time of writing, the pair trades at 1.3531, slightly down 0.34%.

articles.title: AUD/USD falls ahead of RBA decision, Middle East noise limits upside
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair is trading with a cautious tone, hovering near the 0.7190 price zone as investors position ahead of the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) monetary policy decision due later on Monday.

articles.title: Drone or missile strikes reported in the UAE
articles.expand: A short time ago, there were unconfirmed reports of Iranian ballistic missile launches from Isafan province. The speculation at the time is that they were maritime targets. Iran's Press also published videos of Iran firing 'warning shots' at US Destroyers near the Strait of Hormuz earlier today. There are now explosions reported in the Dubai area o...

articles.title: Oil: Two-sided supply risks shape price path – BNY
articles.expand: BNY’s Bob Savage highlights that Oil remains driven by conflicting forces, with U.S. escort plans in the Strait of Hormuz initially knocking prices lower before renewed attacks lifted them again.

articles.title: Gold declines as hawkish Fed bets and firm US Dollar weigh
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USDS) kicks off the week under pressure, hovering near one-month lows as hawkish interest rate expectations continue to weigh on the non-yielding metal amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East.

articles.title: USD/CAD edges higher as US-Iran tensions underpin the US Dollar
articles.expand: USD/CAD edges higher on Monday as rising tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, amid the ongoing US–Iran standoff, support the US Dollar (USD). At the time of writing, the pair is trading around 1.3617, up nearly 0.22% on the day.

articles.title: USD: Data-heavy week shapes Fed view – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities economists Oscar Munoz and Eli Nir anticipate a normalization in United States (US) labor data, with nonfarm payrolls at 80k, unemployment at 4.3% and modest wage growth.

articles.title: EUR/CHF: Short-term bounce, longer-term decline – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Michael Pfister expects EUR/CHF to rise in coming months as the Euro (EUR) recovers and markets push back Swiss National Bank (SNB) rate hikes, with the bank’s strategy combining verbal intervention and steady rates.

articles.title: US Treasury Secretary Bessent: US is opening up Strait of Hormuz
articles.expand: In an interview with Fox News on Monday, US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that the US is opening up the Strait of Hormuz and added that they have "absolute control" of it, per Reuters.

articles.title: Bessent: The world is in an 8-10 million barrel oil supply deficit
articles.expand: I would be interested to see the math on Bessent's calculation on how many barrels are missing. The outlines of it are usually that 20 mbpd goes through the Strait of Hormuz daily under normal conditions. Now that's down to virtually zero, however Saudi Arabia fired up an east-west pipeline to the Red Sea and other mitigation efforts via land have ...

articles.title: Gold Supported by Cautious Optimism
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex Gold is holding at around 4,611 USD per ounce on Monday as markets assess Donald Trump’s proposal to escort commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, alongside tentative signs of progress in US–Iran negotiations. The plan involves assisting civilian ships from neutral countries in safely leaving the conflic...

articles.title: EUR/USD: Dollar seen weakening versus Euro – BNP Paribas
articles.expand: BNP Paribas economists expect the United States (US) economy to grow above potential in 2026, with Gross Domestic Product (GDP) at 2.4% and inflation at 3.3%, while the Fed Funds target range is seen steady at 3.5%-3.75%.

articles.title: Euro area: Weak confidence clouds activity outlook – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists note that firm and consumer confidence fell more than expected in April, with Commission indices at multi‑year lows. Despite slightly positive 1Q Gross Domestic Product (GDP), domestic demand appears subdued and credit conditions are tightening.

articles.title: Oil Shock + Hawkish Fed = Major EUR/USD Downside? Don't Miss These Trading Opportunities
articles.expand: Pressure is building fast on EUR/USD — and two powerful macro forces could send it lower in May. Higher oil prices from Strait of Hormuz disruptions are hammering European growth… while a potentially more hawkish Fed under Kevin Warsh is supercharging the US Dollar. Bearish EUR/USD isn't just a trade on Europe — it's a broad bet on dollar strength....

Markets have largely priced in a 25 basis point move to 4.35%, and the focus has already shifted to what comes next. articles.title: AUD: RBA seen leaning to another hike – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Volkmar Baur highlights strong market conviction that the RBA will deliver a third consecutive rate hike, with futures implying a 75% probability. Inflation and expectations remain well above target and RBA rhetoric has turned more hawkish.

articles.title: EUR/USD: Resilient above 1.17 with ECB split – BNY
articles.expand: BNY’s Bob Savage points out that the Euro is holding above 1.1700 even as the US Dollar (USD) firms, with markets weighing divergent European Central Bank (ECB) rhetoric. François Villeroy de Galhau argues for waiting for more data, while Peter Kažimír signals near-certainty of a June rate hike.

articles.expand: USD/CHF trades around 0.7840 on Monday, up 0.28% on the day, supported by a renewed wave of risk aversion that is boosting the US Dollar (USD).

articles.expand: The Pound (GBP) reverses initial gains against the US Dollar (USD) on Monday and extends its correction from Friday’s highs above 1.3650, to session lows below 1.3550.

Strait of Hormuz closure keeps oil above $100; CPI at 4.1%. articles.title: Tokyo CPI misses forecast sharply, giving BoJ room to hold despite June hike signals
articles.expand: This is a yen-negative, JGB-positive print in the near term. The magnitude of the miss on core-core, coming in at 1.9% against a 2.3% expectation and prior, is significant enough to give the BoJ genuine cover to delay a June hike despite the hawkish signals delivered at the April meeting.
